task: I may need to be away from home for a day or two, and I’m putting together a basic plan so the usual things are covered. What are the main items people typically check before leaving, and what’s worth writing down ahead of time?

turns.agent_response:
Before leaving for a day or two, people usually check these basics:

- **Doors and windows:** locked, closed, and any alarms set
- **Heat/AC:** set to a safe temperature
- **Water:** faucets off, no leaks, and know where the shutoff is if needed
- **Appliances:** stove/oven off, small appliances unplugged if needed
- **Trash and food:** take out trash, put away perishables
- **Mail/packages:** pause deliveries or ask someone to collect them
- **Lights:** leave a light or two on if needed, or use timers
- **Pets/plants:** feeding, water, and care plan
- **Emergency contact:** someone who can reach you and get in if necessary

Worth writing down ahead of time:
- **Who is checking on the place**
- **Their phone number and backup contact**
- **Where keys are kept**
- **Any special instructions** for pets, plants, alarms, or medications
- **Utility shutoff locations** if there’s an emergency
- **Any house rules** for visitors, deliveries, or maintenance while you’re gone
- **What should be done if something goes wrong** and who to call first

If you want, I can turn this into a simple one-page leave-home checklist you can reuse.

user_simulator.text:
Mostly right. Too generic, though.

I’d want the checklist split into two parts: what to do the night before and what to write down in case something happens. And I’d want a few things people always forget:
- stop or hold mail, not just packages
- set the thermostat and note the setting
- unplug anything that could be a nuisance if it fails
- make sure the sink, stove, and windows are actually checked, not just assumed
- leave no obvious sign that nobody’s home

For the written part, I’d want:
- full name and number of the person who has access
- where the spare key is, if there is one
- shutoff valves and breaker panel location
- doctor, pharmacy, and emergency contact numbers if I’m gone more than a day
- a short note on what is normal in the apartment, so someone can tell if something’s off

If you’re going to make it useful, put it in a plain checklist I can print and keep by the door.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plain printable checklist split the way you asked.

# Leave-Home Checklist

## The night before

- [ ] Lock all doors and windows
- [ ] Check windows are actually closed, not just latched
- [ ] Check sink faucets are off
- [ ] Check stove and oven are off
- [ ] Unplug anything that could be a nuisance if it fails
- [ ] Set thermostat to a safe temperature and write down the setting
- [ ] Take out trash
- [ ] Put away perishables
- [ ] Stop or hold mail
- [ ] Pause package delivery or arrange for pickup
- [ ] Leave no obvious sign that nobody is home
- [ ] Set lights/timers as needed
- [ ] Make sure alarms/security systems are set
- [ ] Confirm pets, plants, and any needed care are covered

## Write this down ahead of time

### Access and contacts
- [ ] Full name of the person who has access
- [ ] Phone number of that person
- [ ] Backup contact name and number
- [ ] Where the spare key is, if there is one

### Emergency locations
- [ ] Main water shutoff location
- [ ] Breaker panel location
- [ ] Any other shutoff or utility controls

### Important numbers
- [ ] Doctor
- [ ] Pharmacy
- [ ] Emergency contact

### Notes about the apartment/home
- [ ] Normal thermostat setting
- [ ] What is normal for the place, so someone can tell if something is off
- [ ] Any special instructions for alarms, pets, plants, or medications

If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner one-page version with checkboxes and a blank “notes” section at the bottom.

user_simulator.text:
Better. Still a little thin, but usable.

I’d want one more thing: a spot for the date I leave and when I expect back. And maybe a line for "who to call first" if there’s a problem, because people like to jump around and make a mess of it.

Also, I’d rather not have "backup contact" buried in the middle. Put the access person, spare key, and first-call contact all together. Easier to check when you’re standing at the door with a pen in your hand.

If you can tighten it up into a single page with sections in the order I’d actually use them, that’d be the useful version.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter one-page version in the order you’d actually use it.

# Leave-Home Checklist

## 1) Trip details
- [ ] Date leaving: ____________________
- [ ] Expected return: ____________________
- [ ] Who to call first if there’s a problem: ____________________
- [ ] Phone number: ____________________

## 2) Access
- [ ] Full name of person who has access: ____________________
- [ ] Phone number: ____________________
- [ ] Spare key location, if any: ____________________
- [ ] Backup contact name and number: ____________________

## 3) Night before leaving
- [ ] Lock all doors
- [ ] Check all windows are actually closed
- [ ] Check sink faucets are off
- [ ] Check stove and oven are off
- [ ] Unplug anything that could be a nuisance if it fails
- [ ] Set thermostat: ____________________
- [ ] Take out trash
- [ ] Put away perishables
- [ ] Stop or hold mail
- [ ] Pause package delivery or arrange pickup
- [ ] Leave no obvious sign that nobody is home
- [ ] Set lights/timers as needed
- [ ] Make sure alarms/security systems are set
- [ ] Confirm pets, plants, and any needed care are covered

## 4) Emergency info
- [ ] Main water shutoff location: ____________________
- [ ] Breaker panel location: ____________________
- [ ] Other shutoff/utility controls: ____________________

## 5) Important numbers
- [ ] Doctor: ____________________
- [ ] Pharmacy: ____________________
- [ ] Emergency contact: ____________________

## 6) Notes for the person checking the place
- [ ] Normal thermostat setting: ____________________
- [ ] What is normal here, so something unusual stands out: ____________________
- [ ] Special instructions for alarms, pets, plants, or medications: ____________________

If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner print-ready version with boxes aligned and space to handwrite on one page.

user_simulator.text:
That’s the useful version. Put the whole thing on one page, plain and clean, and I can use it.

If you want to polish it further, make sure the writing space isn’t cramped. That’s where these things go bad on paper.
